text: A 3-centimeter incision was made in the 6 intercostal space in the anterior position.
After selective front lung ventilation, an incomplete fissure was found.
The mass was identified by digital palpation.
The first step of the procedure was the section of the incomplete fissure.
Then the common basal artery was dissected in the fissure and it was divided using an endostepler.
Inferior pulmonary ligament was released The inferior pulmonary vein was identified The vein was dissected and divided
The left lower lobe bronchus was identified and peribronchial tissue was cleared.
The bronchus was divided using an endostepler.
The loop was removed in a protective bag and a steamed lap notice section finished the procedure.
A chest tube was placed via the posterior portion of the incision and the second incision was closed.
The chest tube was removed on postoperative day 2.
The patient was discharged on postoperative day 4 without complications.
